title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Consultation on safety measures for the use of puberty blockers in young people with gender-related health needs Publication date: 21 November 2024 The consultation on safety measures for the use of puberty blockers in young people with gender-related health needs closed on 20 January 2025. We will now consider submissions. Consultation Gender-affirming care Medicine issues Youth health

## Consultation on safety measures for the use of puberty blockers in young people with gender-related health needs
The consultation on safety measures for the use of puberty blockers in young people with gender-related health needs closed on 20 January 2025. We will now consider submissions.
- Gender-affirming care
- Position Statement on the Use of Puberty Blockers in Gender-Affirming Care Publication date: 21 November 2024 This position statement accompanied the release of an evidence brief which examines the safety and long-term impacts of puberty blockers when used in the context of gender-affirming care. This statement summarises the brief’s findings and sets out the Ministry of Health’s expectations for their use. Position statement Medicine issues Gender-affirming care Youth health

## Position Statement on the Use of Puberty Blockers in Gender-Affirming Care
This position statement accompanied the release of an evidence brief which examines the safety and long-term impacts of puberty blockers when used in the context of gender-affirming care. This statement summarises the brief’s findings and sets out the Ministry of Health’s expectations for their use.
- Gender-affirming care
- Impact of Puberty Blockers in Gender-Dysphoric Adolescents: An evidence brief Publication date: 21 November 2024 This literature review summarises the latest national and international evidence of the safety and long-term impacts of puberty blockers on adolescents. Publication Gender-affirming care Youth health Medicine issues

## Impact of Puberty Blockers in Gender-Dysphoric Adolescents: An evidence brief
This literature review summarises the latest national and international evidence of the safety and long-term impacts of puberty blockers on adolescents.
- Gender-affirming care
- Review of the End of Life Choice Act 2019 Publication date: 20 November 2024 This publication reports on the findings of the first review of the End of Life Choice Act 2019, which took place in 2024. Publication Assisted dying

## Review of the End of Life Choice Act 2019
This publication reports on the findings of the first review of the End of Life Choice Act 2019, which took place in 2024.
- Summary of Online Submissions Received on the End of Life Choice Act 2019 Publication date: 20 November 2024 This publication provides a summary of public submissions on what changes could be made to the End of Life Choice Act 2019, as part of the review of the End of Life Choice Act that took place in 2024. Publication Assisted dying

## Summary of Online Submissions Received on the End of Life Choice Act 2019
This publication provides a summary of public submissions on what changes could be made to the End of Life Choice Act 2019, as part of the review of the End of Life Choice Act that took place in 2024.
- Annual Update of Key Results 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 19 November 2024 The Annual Data Explorer presents results from the 2023/24 New Zealand Health Survey, with comparisons to earlier surveys where possible. Publication Alcohol Asian health Child health Diabetes Disability Drugs Māori health Mental health Nutrition Pacific health Obesity Older people (65+) Oral health Socioeconomic deprivation Statistics Surveys Tobacco

## Annual Update of Key Results 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey
The Annual Data Explorer presents results from the 2023/24 New Zealand Health Survey, with comparisons to earlier surveys where possible.
- Socioeconomic deprivation
- Methodology Report 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 19 November 2024 This methodology report details the procedures and protocols followed to ensure the New Zealand Health Survey produces the high-quality and robust data expected of official statistics. Publication Surveys

## Methodology Report 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey
This methodology report details the procedures and protocols followed to ensure the New Zealand Health Survey produces the high-quality and robust data expected of official statistics.
- Questionnaires and Content Guide 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 19 November 2024 The content guide describes the content of the New Zealand Health Survey for the year ended 30 June 2024. Data was collected between July 2023 and July 2024. Publication Surveys

## Questionnaires and Content Guide 2023/24: New Zealand Health Survey
The content guide describes the content of the New Zealand Health Survey for the year ended 30 June 2024. Data was collected between July 2023 and July 2024.
- Pacific Islands Families Study: Young Adult Gambling Behaviours and Associated Risk Factors Publication date: 14 November 2024 In 2000, a cohort of 1,398 Pacific infants, born in South Auckland, was recruited into the Pacific Islands Families (PIF) longitudinal study. In this study, the PIF cohort, aged 22 years, was surveyed about their gambling behaviours. Publication Gambling research Gambling harm

## Pacific Islands Families Study: Young Adult Gambling Behaviours and Associated Risk Factors
In 2000, a cohort of 1,398 Pacific infants, born in South Auckland, was recruited into the Pacific Islands Families (PIF) longitudinal study. In this study, the PIF cohort, aged 22 years, was surveyed about their gambling behaviours.
- Special Patients and Restricted Patients: Guidelines for Regional Forensic Mental Health Services Publication date: 11 November 2024 These guidelines are intended to foster consistent decision-making, facilitate administration, and provide transparency around processes for clinicians responsible for the care and treatment of special patients and restricted patients within the legislative framework of the Mental Health (Compulsory Assessment and Treatment) Act 1992 and the Criminal Procedure (Mentally Impaired Persons) Act 2003. Publication Mental health

## Special Patients and Restricted Patients: Guidelines for Regional Forensic Mental Health Services
These guidelines are intended to foster consistent decision-making, facilitate administration, and provide transparency around processes for clinicians responsible for the care and treatment of special patients and restricted patients within the legislative framework of the Mental Health (Compulsory Assessment and Treatment) Act 1992 and the Criminal Procedure (Mentally Impaired Persons) Act 2003.
- Unlocking the Potential of Active Ageing Publication date: 5 November 2024 The Ministry of Health is inviting written feedback on the proposed topic of its Long-term Insights Briefing – Unlocking the potential of active ageing: Creating health-promoting and supportive environments to empower older people and enhance their contributions to society. Consultation Older people (65+)

## Unlocking the Potential of Active Ageing
The Ministry of Health is inviting written feedback on the proposed topic of its Long-term Insights Briefing – Unlocking the potential of active ageing: Creating health-promoting and supportive environments to empower older people and enhance their contributions to society.
- Strategic Intentions 2024–2028 Publication date: 22 October 2024 The Strategic Intentions 2024–2028 informs Parliament and the public about the Ministry of Health – Manatū Hauora and its role as the chief steward of health and the health system, and the lead advisor to Government on health. Publication About the Ministry Health system

## Strategic Intentions 2024–2028
The Strategic Intentions 2024–2028 informs Parliament and the public about the Ministry of Health – Manatū Hauora and its role as the chief steward of health and the health system, and the lead advisor to Government on health.
- Ministry of Health Annual Report for the year ended 30 June 2024 Publication date: 22 October 2024 The 2023/24 Annual Report summarises the Ministry’s progress towards our Strategic Intentions, and reports on our financial and non-financial performance for 2023/24 as required under the Public Finance Act 1989. Publication About the Ministry

## Ministry of Health Annual Report for the year ended 30 June 2024
The 2023/24 Annual Report summarises the Ministry’s progress towards our Strategic Intentions, and reports on our financial and non-financial performance for 2023/24 as required under the Public Finance Act 1989.
- Health National Adaptation Plan 2024 - 2027 Publication date: 22 October 2024 The Health National Adaptation Plan (HNAP) sets the strategic direction and provides national-level priority actions for health-focused adaptation to climate change. Publication Environmental health Public health

## Health National Adaptation Plan 2024 - 2027
The Health National Adaptation Plan (HNAP) sets the strategic direction and provides national-level priority actions for health-focused adaptation to climate change.
- Environmental health
- Drug Checking Licensing Scheme Testing Methods Amendment Form Publication date: 26 September 2024 Complete this form for any requested additions of drug checking testing methods to a provider’s licence. Publication Drug checking

## Drug Checking Licensing Scheme Testing Methods Amendment Form
Complete this form for any requested additions of drug checking testing methods to a provider’s licence.
